description: Gameplay UX polish: dark-mode-first, mobile-first UI, clearer lobby/game list rows, stronger in-game card rendering (names + side values), unskippable archetype selection, leave/forfeit confirmation, and better capture feedback. Includes small API extensions (player email, completion reason, cards endpoint) to keep the UI snapshot-first.

userStories.title: Dark mode default + mobile-first layout baseline
userStories.description: As a player, I want the UI to feel good on mobile and in dark mode so the game is comfortable to play.
acceptanceCriteria: App renders in dark mode by default (no manual toggle required)
Update /games and /g/:gameId layouts to be mobile-first (spacing, stacking, widths)
Clickable elements (buttons, cards, board cells) show correct hover/disabled states and cursors
Existing unit/e2e tests still pass (update expectations only when required)
userStories.notes: Keep it incremental: adjust tokens + layout primitives first, then refine per-screen.

userStories.title: API: expose participant emails in GameState
userStories.description: As a UI client, I want player display identities (email) so the lobby and game room can say who I'm playing.
acceptanceCriteria: JWT validation exposes caller email in addition to user_id (sub)
GameState includes optional player email fields for participants
POST /games and POST /games/:gameId/join record the caller email into the snapshot
GET /games and GET /games/:gameId return these email fields
Do not weaken spectator blocking: non-participants still cannot read ACTIVE/COMPLETE snapshots
userStories.notes: Prefer storing email in snapshot so listGames can render without extra DB joins.

userStories.title: API: record completion reason (normal vs forfeit) for result labels
userStories.description: As a player, I want completed games to show Win/Loss/Forfeit accurately.
acceptanceCriteria: GameState exposes enough result metadata to distinguish normal wins from forfeits
Leaving an ACTIVE game sets completion metadata (who forfeited) in the snapshot
Normal (non-forfeit) completions keep existing win/lose/draw behavior
Add/adjust tests to cover the new result metadata
userStories.notes: Keep backwards-compatible JSON fields where possible; the web UI will use the new metadata.

userStories.title: Archetype selection is an unskippable modal (and moves are blocked until chosen)
userStories.description: As a player, I must choose an archetype when the match starts so the game loop is consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: When the game is ACTIVE and the caller has no archetype, show a blocking modal dialog to choose one
Disable move interactions (hand selection and board placement) until archetype is selected
Backend rejects move submission for a player with no archetype (422 with actionable detail)
Add/adjust tests covering the blocking behavior (API + UI)
userStories.notes: Do not rely on UI-only gating; enforce on the backend too.

userStories.title: Game room header UX: opponent title + back link; remove Leave after completion
userStories.description: As a player, I want clear navigation and context while playing.
acceptanceCriteria: Game title reads: Playing against <opponent email> (or Waiting for opponent)
Add a non-forfeit navigation link back to /games from /g/:gameId
After game completion, remove the Leave Game button (keep navigation back to /games)
Buttons show proper hover/disabled states and cursors
userStories.notes: Leaving a completed game should just be navigation; no extra API call needed.

userStories.title: Leaving ACTIVE games requires confirmation (forfeit warning dialog)
userStories.description: As a player, I want a clear warning before forfeiting so I don't misclick.
acceptanceCriteria: In ACTIVE games, clicking Leave shows a confirmation dialog explaining it will forfeit
Cancel keeps the player in the game
Confirm triggers the leave/forfeit call and returns to /games
WAITING lobby leave remains fast (no forfeit language)
userStories.notes: Prefer a shadcn/ui Dialog so keyboard focus and escape behavior are correct by default.

userStories.title: Games list UX: opponent + truncated id + Win/Loss/Forfeit labels
userStories.description: As a player, I want the games list to be scannable so I can quickly resume matches.
acceptanceCriteria: Each row shows opponent email (or Waiting...) plus a truncated game id
Rows show status and, when COMPLETE, a result label for the current viewer: Win/Loss/Forfeit/Draw
Avoid showing the full UUID by default (still accessible via copy or secondary text if desired)
Row and button interactions have clear hover/disabled feedback and correct cursors
userStories.notes: Truncation target: 8 chars is fine (GitHub-ish), but keep full id available somewhere for debugging.

userStories.title: API: add GET /cards for UI card rendering
userStories.description: As a UI client, I want card definitions (name + side values) so gameplay is readable.
acceptanceCriteria: Add GET /cards endpoint returning card_key, name/version (display), and sides n/e/s/w
Frontend adds a small card-definition fetch/cache layer
Hand and board rendering can resolve card_key -> display fields
Add/adjust tests for the new endpoint
userStories.notes: Use the server's CardStore as the source so UI stays snapshot-first and deterministic.

userStories.title: Card visuals: render name + side values on board and in hand (with hover enlargement)
userStories.description: As a player, I need to see card stats clearly so I can make decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: Cards render display name (not card_key) plus numeric side values (N/E/S/W) in both hand and board
Hovering a hand card improves readability (background change + slight scale/enlarge)
Selection state remains clear (selected vs unselected, disabled vs enabled)
Mobile-first sizing keeps cards legible without horizontal scrolling
userStories.notes: Keep implementation lightweight (Tailwind + existing shadcn primitives).

userStories.title: Capture feedback: clear indication of flips/combos after a move
userStories.description: As a player, I want immediate feedback when captures occur so I understand what happened.
acceptanceCriteria: When captures occur, show a clear per-move indicator (count and/or which cards flipped)
Combos feel distinct from single flips (reuse capturedCells diff, no client-side rules)
Effects work in dark mode and remain readable on mobile
Buttons and interactive elements have consistent hover/disabled/cursor feedback
userStories.notes: Prefer using the existing board diff logic; only add new snapshot fields if truly needed.

userStories.title: API: enrich last_move snapshot details (who/what/where)
userStories.description: As a UI client, I want authoritative last-move details so I can highlight and describe the last action without guessing.
acceptanceCriteria: GameState.last_move includes: player_index (0/1), card_key, and cell_index (0-8) in addition to mists_roll and mists_effect
Move submission updates these fields for every successful placement
Sudden Death and archetype usage do not break last_move population
Add/adjust tests to assert the new last_move fields are present and correct
userStories.notes: Keep this snapshot-first; do not add client-side rules.

userStories.title: Last move highlight + text callout in game room
userStories.description: As a player, I want to quickly understand what just happened.
acceptanceCriteria: Game room highlights the last placed cell for a short period after updates
Show a short callout line using snapshot data (e.g., 'Opponent played <Name>')
Works on mobile and in dark mode
No client-side inference: all details come from snapshot/definitions
userStories.notes: Requires US-UX-011 and US-UX-008/US-UX-009 for names.

userStories.title: Realtime status indicator + manual refresh/resync
userStories.description: As a player, I want to know if live updates are working and be able to resync when they aren't.
acceptanceCriteria: Show a small connection state indicator (Live / Reconnecting) in the game room
Provide a manual Refresh action that refetches GET /games/:gameId
When realtime disconnects, keep the existing interval refetch fallback and surface the state
Update/extend tests where practical (at least unit-level state rendering)
userStories.notes: Keep it minimal; no complicated reconnection logic beyond what exists.

userStories.title: Mobile card inspect: tap-to-preview for hand and board
userStories.description: As a mobile player, I need a way to inspect cards since hover effects don't exist on touch.
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping a card (hand or board) opens a readable preview (dialog/sheet) with name + N/E/S/W
Preview is accessible (focus trap, close button, escape key)
Does not interfere with move placement UX (placing still requires explicit selection + cell tap)
Works well in dark mode
userStories.notes: Use shadcn Dialog/Sheet; keep the component reusable.

userStories.title: Accessibility and interaction polish pass
userStories.description: As a player, I want the UI to be usable with keyboard and readable with good contrast.
acceptanceCriteria: Interactive elements have visible focus styles (keyboard navigation works across key screens)
Color/contrast remains readable in dark mode (text and status indicators)
Hand/board interactions are operable without a mouse where feasible
Update/add a small unit test to prevent regression (e.g., presence of focusable controls)
userStories.notes: Keep this as a bounded pass; avoid redesigning everything.
